waypoints.note: Bivouac and assembly areas south and east of Caen (Falaise–Saint-Pierre-sur-Dives axis). Centroid south of Caen at the start of the night.

disputedBy.claim: Feuchtinger's authority to commit the division was constrained by the OKW reserve rule. Sources differ on the exact moment release came through (early morning per German staff records vs ~14:00 per some Allied accounts) and on whether Feuchtinger acted on his own initiative against elements east of the Orne before formal release.

waypoints.note: Still south of Caen at H-Hour. Some elements (notably Pz-Aufkl-Abt 21) were already engaging the British 6th Airborne east of the Orne overnight on local initiative.
Division ordered north through Caen toward the British landings ; movement slowed by Allied air interdiction and the Caen road bottleneck.
Counterattack assembly north of Caen, Pz-Rgt 22 forming up between Lebisey and Bieville to attack toward the coast.

disputedBy.claim: The narrative claim that elements of 21. Panzer 'reached the sea' between Sword and Juno is contested. A small Kampfgruppe Rauch (Pz-Gren-Rgt 192) did make it to Lion-sur-Mer briefly in the evening, but the bulk of the panzer regiment was repulsed by Sherman Fireflies at Bieville and never closed the coastal gap.

waypoints.note: Counterattack toward Lion-sur-Mer in progress. KG Rauch reaches the coast briefly before withdrawing ; main Pz-Rgt 22 stalled at Bieville/Lebisey.
End of MVP window: division line stabilises along the Lebisey–Bieville ridge, blocking the British 3rd ID's drive on Caen. Position is divisional centroid, approximate.
